steps.title: Fastest path: identify complements first
steps.content: Label the named acute angles first. Then use the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Note that angles N and P are complementary
steps.content: Triangle MNP has $\angle M = 90^\circ$, $\angle N = 64^\circ$, $\angle P = 26^\circ$. The two acute angles in any right triangle sum to $90^\circ$, and indeed $64^\circ + 26^\circ = 90^\circ$, so $N$ and $P$ are complementary.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity
steps.content: For complementary angles, $\sin(\theta) = \cos(90^\circ - \theta)$. Since $P = 90^\circ - N$, we get $\cos P = \cos(90^\circ - N) = \sin N$. Numerically, $\sin 64^\circ \approx 0.8988$ and $\cos 26^\circ \approx 0.8988$ — they are equal.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content: The values are equal, so the correct statement is $\cos P=\sin N$. That matches Choice B — $\cos P = \sin N$.
